title: About — FAQ
summary: Common questions about About.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is About?
A: About — detected from /about.

Q: Where do I find About?
A: In the app at /about.

Q: How do I use About?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see About?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

sections.heading: Who it’s for
body: Built for players, parents, and coaches.

sections.heading: Why it matters
body: It turns something you’d otherwise have to figure out into a few clear steps you can act on today.

sections.heading: When to use it
body: In the app at /about.
Use it whenever you want the outcome this feature provides.

sections.heading: Step by step
body: 1. Open About — Go to /about.
2. See what’s on screen — About is where you about — detected from /about.
3. Take the main action — Use the primary control to run the feature — the screen guides you through it.
4. Review the result — Read what comes back. Anything based on visual analysis is a smart starting point, not a measurement.
5. Save or come back — Your work is tied to your private account and synced across devices, so you can pick up later.

sections.heading: Common mistakes
body: Skipping the on-screen guidance and guessing.
Expecting a perfect score on day one — each result is a confident starting point to build on.

sections.heading: Best practices
body: Do one focused thing at a time.
Come back regularly so your progress is comparable over time.

title: Academy — admin guide
summary: Operate Academy: setup, permissions, monitoring, escalation.

sections.heading: Setup
body: In the admin dashboard at /admin/academy/advisor.
No special setup is required beyond admin access.

sections.heading: Permissions & roles
body: Any admin role can access this; scope with RBAC if needed.

sections.heading: Configuration
body: No dedicated configuration; uses platform defaults.

sections.heading: Monitoring
body: Watch usage in Analytics and errors in System Health.
Spot-check outputs for quality before relying on them.

sections.heading: Data management
body: Operates on existing account data; no dedicated store to manage.

sections.heading: Audit log
body: Admin actions here are recorded in the audit log; review it after changes.

sections.heading: Support escalation
body: Tier 1: support enablement doc. Tier 2: admin review. Tier 3: engineering with route + error id.

sections.heading: Failure states
body: If the feature errors, it should degrade safely (honest empty/error state) rather than block the page.

sections.heading: Operational risks
body: Low operational risk for normal use.

title: Analytics — admin guide
summary: Operate Analytics: setup, permissions, monitoring, escalation.

sections.heading: Setup
body: In the admin dashboard at /admin/analytics.
No special setup is required beyond admin access.

sections.heading: Permissions & roles
body: Any admin role can access this; scope with RBAC if needed.

sections.heading: Configuration
body: No dedicated configuration; uses platform defaults.

sections.heading: Monitoring
body: Watch usage in Analytics and errors in System Health.
Spot-check outputs for quality before relying on them.

sections.heading: Data management
body: Operates on existing account data; no dedicated store to manage.

sections.heading: Audit log
body: Admin actions here are recorded in the audit log; review it after changes.

sections.heading: Support escalation
body: Tier 1: support enablement doc. Tier 2: admin review. Tier 3: engineering with route + error id.

sections.heading: Failure states
body: If the feature errors, it should degrade safely (honest empty/error state) rather than block the page.

sections.heading: Operational risks
body: Low operational risk for normal use.

body: In the admin dashboard at /admin/insights.
No special setup is required beyond admin access.

sections.heading: Permissions & roles
body: Any admin role can access this; scope with RBAC if needed.

sections.heading: Configuration
body: No dedicated configuration; uses platform defaults.

sections.heading: Monitoring
body: Watch usage in Analytics and errors in System Health.
Spot-check outputs for quality before relying on them.

sections.heading: Data management
body: Operates on existing account data; no dedicated store to manage.

sections.heading: Audit log
body: Admin actions here are recorded in the audit log; review it after changes.

sections.heading: Support escalation
body: Tier 1: support enablement doc. Tier 2: admin review. Tier 3: engineering with route + error id.

sections.heading: Failure states
body: If the feature errors, it should degrade safely (honest empty/error state) rather than block the page.

sections.heading: Operational risks
body: Low operational risk for normal use.

title: Agi — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Agi.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Agi?
A: Agi — detected from /agi.

Q: Where do I find Agi?
A: In the app at /agi.

Q: How do I use Agi?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Agi?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: How to use AI Coach
summary: A plain-language walkthrough of AI Coach for people new to SwingVantage.

sections.heading: What it does
body: AI Coach — detected from /ai-coach.

sections.heading: Who it’s for
body: Built for people new to SwingVantage, returning athletes.

sections.heading: Why it matters
body: It turns something you’d otherwise have to figure out into a few clear steps you can act on today.

sections.heading: When to use it
body: In the app at /ai-coach.
Use it whenever you want the outcome this feature provides.

sections.heading: Step by step
body: 1. Open AI Coach — Go to /ai-coach.
2. See what’s on screen — AI Coach is where you aI Coach — detected from /ai-coach.
3. Take the main action — Use the primary control to run the feature — the screen guides you through it.
4. Review the result — Read what comes back. Anything based on visual analysis is a smart starting point, not a measurement.
5. Save or come back — Your work is tied to your private account and synced across devices, so you can pick up later.

sections.heading: Common mistakes
body: Skipping the on-screen guidance and guessing.
Expecting a perfect score on day one — each result is a confident starting point to build on.

sections.heading: Best practices
body: Do one focused thing at a time.
Come back regularly so your progress is comparable over time.

sections.heading: What you’ll get
body: A clear, saved result you can build on and revisit.

sections.heading: Related & next
body: Open it directly: /ai-coach
See the full release note for this feature for what changed and why.
Browse the Tutorial Center for related walkthroughs.

title: Admin — admin guide
summary: Operate Admin: setup, permissions, monitoring, escalation.

sections.heading: Setup
body: Via the API endpoint /api/admin/ai/user-cap.
No special setup is required beyond admin access.

sections.heading: Permissions & roles
body: Any admin role can access this; scope with RBAC if needed.

sections.heading: Configuration
body: No dedicated configuration; uses platform defaults.

sections.heading: Monitoring
body: Watch usage in Analytics and errors in System Health.
Spot-check outputs for quality before relying on them.

sections.heading: Data management
body: Operates on existing account data; no dedicated store to manage.

sections.heading: Audit log
body: Admin actions here are recorded in the audit log; review it after changes.

sections.heading: Support escalation
body: Tier 1: support enablement doc. Tier 2: admin review. Tier 3: engineering with route + error id.

sections.heading: Failure states
body: If the feature errors, it should degrade safely (honest empty/error state) rather than block the page.

sections.heading: Operational risks
body: Low operational risk for normal use.

title: Arc — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Arc.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Arc?
A: Arc — detected from /arc.

Q: Where do I find Arc?
A: In the app at /arc.

Q: How do I use Arc?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Arc?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: Design Lab — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Design Lab.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Design Lab?
A: Design Lab — detected from /design-lab/dashboard.

Q: Where do I find Design Lab?
A: In the app at /design-lab/dashboard.

Q: How do I use Design Lab?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Design Lab?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions

Q: What is How It Works?

Q: Where do I find How It Works?

A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see How It Works?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?

A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: Mental — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Mental.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Mental?
A: Mental — detected from /mental/journal.

Q: Where do I find Mental?
A: In the app at /mental/journal.

Q: How do I use Mental?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Mental?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: Motion Lab — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Motion Lab.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Motion Lab?
A: Motion Lab — detected from /motion-lab.

Q: Where do I find Motion Lab?
A: In the app at /motion-lab.

Q: How do I use Motion Lab?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Motion Lab?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: Practice — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Practice.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Practice?
A: Practice — detected from /practice.

Q: Where do I find Practice?
A: In the app at /practice.

Q: How do I use Practice?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Practice?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: Profile — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Profile.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Profile?
A: Profile — detected from /profile.

Q: Where do I find Profile?
A: In the app at /profile.

Q: How do I use Profile?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Profile?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

title: Resources — FAQ
summary: Common questions about Resources.

sections.heading: Frequently asked questions
body:
Q: What is Resources?
A: Resources — detected from /resources.

Q: Where do I find Resources?
A: In the app at /resources.

Q: How do I use Resources?
A: Open it, take the main action the screen guides you to, and review your saved result. See the tutorial for step-by-step help.

Q: Why don’t I see Resources?
A: Make sure you’re signed in. Some features appear only after you’ve added your sport or first session.

Q: What permissions do I need?
A: No special permissions are required.

Q: What data does it use?
A: Your account data and what you provide on the screen. Your data is tied to your private account and synced across devices.

Q: What if it doesn’t work?
A: See the troubleshooting guide for this feature, or contact support from the in-app help menu.

Q: Can I edit, delete, or export what I create?
A: Yes — your results live in your account and can be managed from the Data center.

Expecting a perfect score on day one — each result is a confident starting point to build on.

sections.heading: Best practices
body: Do one focused thing at a time.
Come back regularly so your progress is comparable over time.

sections.heading: What you’ll get
body: A clear, saved result you can build on and revisit.

steps.detail: Your work is tied to your private account and synced across devices, so you can pick up later.
